text: checkout keeps timing out for people paying with mada cards. basically anyone in saudi using mada (thats like 60% of our KSA customers, KSA is 30% of revenue) gets a spinner for 40+ seconds then error. support says ~200 tickets last week about this. we lose the cart contents too so they have to start over. probably the 3DS redirect handler but not sure

payment page dies for UPI users in india. every customer paying through UPI (most of our india volume, india is our #2 market) waits forever and then gets a generic failure. cs team counted 150ish complaints this month. cart gets wiped as well so they abandon. think its the callback url handling but havent confirmed

the nightly invoice export job silently skips invoices with multibyte customer names. finance found 340 missing invoices last quarter, thats real money (~AED 90k unbilled). affects every customer with arabic or chinese names which is a big chunk of enterprise accounts. no error anywhere, the job says success. need to fix the encoding handling in the csv writer i think

weekly payroll sync drops employees whose names have accents. HR noticed 85 people didnt get overtime processed in march, about $40k underpaid in total. hits basically all our french and brazilian staff. the sync reports completed fine, no warnings. probably string normalization in the upload step but not verified

search on the help center returns nothing for misspelled queries. data shows 4 out of 10 searches have a typo, those users see zero results and either spam support chat (support costs went up 18% since launch) or just leave. competitors handle this fine. we need fuzzy matching or at least a did-you-mean. not sure which search lib version were even on

product search in the mobile app gives empty results if you spell something slightly wrong. analytics says roughly a third of mobile searches contain typos and those sessions convert at basically 0%, vs 4% normally. people then call the hotline which costs us per call. need typo tolerance, no idea how the current index is configured

password reset emails take 25-40 min to arrive. every locked out user is stuck that whole time. its the single biggest driver of support calls per the april report (31% of all calls) and b2b admins escalate it to their account managers which makes us look bad in renewals. mail queue is probably backed up behind marketing sends, they share a queue i believe

OTP sms for login shows up after 10-20 minutes sometimes. user cant get in at all until it lands. biggest complaint category in the q1 support review (around 25% of tickets) and enterprise customers bring it up in QBRs. guessing the sms provider queue is shared with notification blasts but someone should check

admin dashboard loads ALL transactions into memory to render the summary tiles. for our 3 biggest tenants (together 45% of ARR) the page takes 50+ sec or just OOMs. their ops teams use this page every morning, two of them mentioned it in churn-risk calls. needs pagination or a precomputed summary table. code is in the legacy reporting module somewhere

the orders overview screen fetches every order ever to draw the kpi widgets. our two largest accounts (over a third of revenue) see 1 minute load times or browser crashes. their managers open this screen daily and one flagged it as a reason theyre evaluating alternatives. should aggregate server side. its somewhere in the old analytics package

csv import dies without any message when theres a blank row in the file. onboarding team says 7 of 10 new customers hit this in week one because excel adds trailing blanks, and each failure means a support call and a day lost in onboarding (time-to-value metric got flagged by leadership). just skip blank rows and show a summary. importer code, wherever that lives

bulk contact upload fails with no feedback if any phone number has spaces in it. basically every customer migrating from the old system has this (sales says it came up in 8 recent onboardings) and each one needs an engineer to clean the file, adds ~2 days to go-live which procurement teams notice. should just normalize numbers and report what was fixed. in the migration tool code

session expires after 15 min and kicks nurses out mid-form in the patient intake app, they lose everything typed. ward staff fill 30-40 of these per shift, the head nurse at our biggest hospital client said staff keep paper notes now and type later which defeats the whole point and risks transcription errors. either autosave drafts or extend session for active users. dont know what the session middleware is doing exactly

the inspection app logs field engineers out after 10 minutes idle and throws away the half done checklist. crews do 20+ inspections a day, the ops lead at our largest utility customer says techs photograph the screen as backup which is a data protection problem honestly. need draft saving or activity based session refresh. not sure where timeout is set

webhook retries hammer customer endpoints when they have an outage. one customer came back from 10 min downtime to 40k queued retries that took their service down AGAIN, they threatened to drop the integration (theyre our 4th largest). no backoff at all right now, just retry every 5 sec forever. need exponential backoff + a cap. webhook dispatcher code

our sync agent floods partner APIs after they recover from maintenance. a key integration partner (drives maybe 15% of new signups) got 25k calls in 5 minutes post-maintenance and rate limited us for a week, breaking sync for all shared customers. theres no backoff just a tight retry loop. add jittered exponential backoff. in the connector framework

mobile app re-downloads the full product catalog (30mb) on every launch. users on prepaid data plans (most of our african and south asian markets, fastest growing segment per the board deck) burn through data and uninstall - play store reviews mention data usage constantly, rating dropped 4.4 to 3.9. need delta sync or at least cache headers. api side and app side both involved probably

the field sales app pulls the entire 25mb price book every time it opens. reps in markets with expensive mobile data (latam mainly, our growth bet this year) complain constantly and several switched back to the excel sheet, app store rating fell half a star this quarter citing data drain. should sync only changes. touches the sync service and the app cache layer

error page shows a raw stack trace including db connection string pieces when the reporting service is down. happened during last tuesdays outage, a customer screenshotted it and posted it in a public forum, security team had to rotate credentials. anyone hitting reports during any outage sees this. need a proper error page and to scrub exception output. error handler in the reports gateway

when the billing service times out the invoice page dumps the full exception with internal hostnames and a partial api key visible. compliance flagged it in the soc2 audit prep as a finding we must close. every user on the billing page during a blip sees it. swap in a friendly error and sanitize the handler. billing frontend error boundary plus api error middleware

report scheduling is off by the server timezone not the users. customers in australia get their monday morning report sunday afternoon, asia pacific customers (20% of base, target segment for expansion) complain the numbers cut off mid-business-day so month end reports are wrong for them. store user tz and schedule against it. scheduler module and probably a migration for existing schedules

automated statements run on UTC midnight regardless of customer location. gulf region banks (priority vertical, 3 of our top 10 accounts) get statements that split their business day in half so their reconciliation team manually adjusts every day, one called it a blocker for expanding usage. need per-tenant timezone for the statement cutoff. statement generator plus backfill for current tenants

deleting a team member doesnt revoke their api tokens. ex employee of a customer kept pulling data for 3 weeks after offboarding, the customer found it in their audit and was furious, legal got involved. affects every customer that rotates staff. tokens should die with the user, simple as that. auth service, token table has no fk to users apparently

removing a user from an org leaves their mobile sessions alive indefinitely. a contractor for one of our enterprise clients still had read access for a month after termination, came up in the clients security review and nearly killed the renewal. hits any org with staff turnover ie all of them. sessions must be invalidated on removal. session store doesnt index by user id which is why nobody built it

duplicate notifications, sometimes 5x for the same event. power users (the ones who actually drive seat expansion) turn notifications off entirely, then miss real alerts and complain we never warned them. notif team said its a race in the fanout worker when retries overlap. dedupe by event id at delivery time. been open for two quarters now

alert emails fire 3-6 times for one incident. on call engineers at customer sites mute the alert channel because of it, and then miss actual outages - one customer missed a real incident and blamed our noise, it came up in the renewal call. cause is concurrent workers claiming the same alert row. need idempotent delivery keyed on incident id. has been reported on and off for a year

the public status page is hosted on the same infra as the product. when we go down it goes down too lol. during the march outage customers had zero information for 90 min, twitter filled the gap badly, and 3 enterprise prospects asked about it in security questionnaires since. move it to separate hosting, its a static page basically. small task huge optics

health check endpoint lives behind the same load balancer it is supposed to monitor. when the lb has problems our monitoring says everything is fine, which is how the february incident ran 40 minutes before anyone noticed - customers told US about it. sre says move the probe external, its a config change mostly. embarrassing in postmortems twice now

free trial signup asks for a credit card upfront. growth ran the numbers, we lose roughly 55% of signups at that step vs competitors who dont ask, and the segment we lose most is exactly the self serve smb motion the company bet on this year. make card optional and gate at trial end instead. billing integration and the signup flow, plus some dunning logic for trial conversion

demo booking form requires a company phone number and vat id. marketing measured a 48% drop off on that page, mostly small agencies which is the exact icp for the new pricing tier launching next quarter. ask for email only and collect the rest later. form is in the marketing site repo, crm mapping needs a tweak too
